channel.title: Comments on: Genesee Brewing: Bock Beer, Ep. 142
channel.description: Beer America TV is the first beer web site dedicated to the specialty craft brewing world with video on industry experts and insiders plus more!

item.title: By: Scooter Hendon
item.dc:creator: Scooter Hendon
item.description: Yeah, Shiner Bock isn't actually a bock, but more of an American Dark Lager, if we're being technical about it.
item.content:encoded: Yeah, Shiner Bock isn’t actually a bock, but more of an American Dark Lager, if we’re being technical about it.

item.title: By: Video BrewMaster
item.dc:creator: Video BrewMaster
item.description: Genesee brews decent beer overall, nothing earth shattering, but very drinkable brews. The Bock and Cream Ale are good beers in my opinion. The connection to Sam Adams is that they contract brew for them - in other words, the Sam Adams you buy in that region is actually brewed at the Genesee Brewing Company. In truth, Sam Adams does this with breweries all over the country.
item.content:encoded: Genesee brews decent beer overall, nothing earth shattering, but very drinkable brews. The Bock and Cream Ale are good beers in my opinion. The connection to Sam Adams is that they contract brew for them – in other words, the Sam Adams you buy in that region is actually brewed at the Genesee Brewing Company. In truth, Sam Adams does this with breweries all over the country.

item.title: By: Dave Porter
item.dc:creator: Dave Porter
item.description: Glad to see the old home town and Genesee getting some positive vibes for local business. It has been many years since my beer drinking days so it would not be fair to assume that Genesee brewery has not made vast improvements. What is the connection between them and Sam Adams?
item.content:encoded: Glad to see the old home town and Genesee getting some positive vibes for local business. It has been many years since my beer drinking days so it would not be fair to assume that Genesee brewery has not made vast improvements. What is the connection between them and Sam Adams?

item.title: By: Scott Coughlin
item.dc:creator: Scott Coughlin
item.description: Love your videos. I enjoy watching you and John play off of one another. John gives his point of views as a Brew Master and you as a "regular guy". Great videos. Keep it up.
item.content:encoded: Love your videos. I enjoy watching you and John play off of one another. John gives his point of views as a Brew Master and you as a “regular guy”. Great videos. Keep it up.

item.title: By: Video BrewMaster
item.dc:creator: Video BrewMaster
item.description:
Great comments Jen. Although I have to disagree with you, I actually think their Cream Ale is an excellent Cream Ale, although truth be told, I was afraid for years to try it when I lived in Rochester. Genny needs marketing help because they do brew a few good beers, but I think most people are stuck on the image that they brew gutter beer. The Bock is excellent, and if you want to know more about the style, check out the previous two episodes we shot dealing with Saint Arnolds and Rahr - we talk more about the style in those.
Thanks for the comment!!!
item.content:encoded:
Great comments Jen. Although I have to disagree with you, I actually think their Cream Ale is an excellent Cream Ale, although truth be told, I was afraid for years to try it when I lived in Rochester. Genny needs marketing help because they do brew a few good beers, but I think most people are stuck on the image that they brew gutter beer. The Bock is excellent, and if you want to know more about the style, check out the previous two episodes we shot dealing with Saint Arnolds and Rahr – we talk more about the style in those.

Thanks for the comment!!!

item.title: By: Jen
item.dc:creator: Jen
item.description: I'm surprised to see Genesee on here and even more surprised to hear positive things about it, considering how terrible Genny Cream Ale is. It is lovely packaging, though! A rather uneven episode though--would like to have heard a clearer explanation on Bock beer.
item.content:encoded: I’m surprised to see Genesee on here and even more surprised to hear positive things about it, considering how terrible Genny Cream Ale is. It is lovely packaging, though! A rather uneven episode though–would like to have heard a clearer explanation on Bock beer.
